Home› 2021› 爷青结

爷青结 — My Youth Has Ended

Pronounced yé qīng jié in Mandarin
2021 fading ★★★☆☆ lifestyle

What Does 爷青结 Mean?

The melancholy counterpart to 爷青回 (my youth is back) — 爷青结 means 'my youth has ended' (结 = concluded). Where 爷青回 celebrates nostalgia returning, 爷青结 marks the definitive end of an era: when a beloved band breaks up, a childhood show gets canceled, a game server shuts down forever. The youth that 爷青回 temporarily revived is now conclusively over.

Cultural Context

The pair 爷青回/爷青结 captures the bittersweet nature of nostalgia — the joy of things returning and the pain of things ending. 爷青结 moments often trigger surprising grief in internet communities, revealing how much emotional weight people invest in cultural products from their formative years.

How Is It Used?

偶像组合解散了，爷青结。
The idol group disbanded. My youth has ended.
游戏停服了，爷青结，再也回不去了。
The game servers are shutting down. My youth has ended — can never go back.

Chinese Explanation (中文解释)

爷青回的反义词，表示某事标志着青春的结束，让人感到时光流逝和怀旧的失落感。

Related Chinese Memes

鸽了
Ghosted / Bailed / Stood Someone Up
gē le
From 放鸽子 (release a pigeon/dove) — the Chinese equivalent of standing someone up. '鸽了' means you bailed, ghosted, didn't show up, or failed to deliver what you promised. It applies to social plans ('I鸽了, sorry'), gaming commitments ('that streamer鸽了 again'), and development promises ('the game has been鸽ing for three years'). The pigeon metaphor is perfect: you released a bird that flew away and never came back.
2017 still popular romancelifestyle ★★★★★
主打一个
The Main Thing Is / Leading With
zhǔ dǎ yī gè
A sentence structure meaning 'the main thing is [X]' or 'leading with [X].' Exploded across Chinese social media as a template for describing the defining quality of anything: 主打一个不在乎 (leading with not caring), 主打一个真实 (the main thing is being real), 主打一个随缘 (going with the flow is the vibe). It's both a description and a statement of intent — this is the energy I'm bringing.
2023 still popular internet-cultureidentity ★★★★★
怼
To Diss / Verbally Smack Down
duì
To directly confront, clap back at, or verbally attack someone head-on. Stronger than criticism, more direct than passive aggression — 怼 is an open verbal collision. '我要怼他' means 'I'm going to go right at them.' Essential for understanding Chinese social media arguments, celebrity feuds, and political commentary. Everyone 怼s everyone on Chinese internet.
2017 still popular lifestyle ★★★★☆
傅园慧
Fu Yuanhui (The Prehistoric Power Swimmer)
fú yuán huì
Chinese swimmer who became a global viral sensation at the 2016 Rio Olympics for her spectacularly unfiltered post-race interviews. Declaring she had used her 'prehistoric power,' making exaggerated faces, and expressing genuine surprise at her results — she was the opposite of the polished, robotic athlete interview. Her authenticity in a culture that prizes composed performance was revolutionary.
2016 classic lifestyle ★★★★☆
